project.name: ccbot
project.description: Telegram Bot for monitoring Claude Code sessions

nodes.name: Agentic Analyze
nodes.summary: Scheduled weekly analysis pipeline (Mondays 06:00 UTC). Runs codebase analysis and backlog ranking via scripts/agentic/cli.py analyze and plan commands. Uploads analysis report, plan, and selected-task artifacts. Triggered on schedule, workflow_dispatch, or pushes to agentic config paths.

nodes.name: Agentic Implement
nodes.summary: Implementation pipeline that selects a backlog task, prepares prompts, creates a feature branch, invokes anthropics/claude-code-action to implement changes, validates results, and opens a draft PR. Supports workflow_dispatch and workflow_call triggers. Has three jobs: prepare (analyze/select/export metadata), implement (branch+claude+validate), and dry-run-summary (preview only). Includes concurrency gating to prevent parallel runs.

nodes.name: Agentic Orchestrator
nodes.summary: Orchestration pipeline that chains analysis, planning, and optional implementation. Runs analyze+select, optionally syncs issues to GitHub, decides whether to chain into agentic-implement.yml based on inputs or repository variable AGENTIC_AUTO_IMPLEMENT. Supports manual dispatch with task_id override and dry-run mode. Scheduled runs default to analysis-only unless auto-implement is enabled.

nodes.name: Agentic Plan
nodes.summary: Manual planning pipeline triggered via workflow_dispatch. Runs codebase analysis and task selection. Optionally syncs backlog items as GitHub issues with priority/category labels. Uploads plan artifacts including selected-task.json and plan.md.

nodes.name: Check
nodes.summary: Standard CI quality gate pipeline. Runs on push and pull_request to main. Executes ruff format check, ruff lint, pyright type checking, and pytest with a Python version matrix (3.12, 3.13). Uses astral-sh/setup-uv for fast Python dependency management.

nodes.name: Claude Code Review
nodes.summary: Automated code review pipeline triggered on pull_request_target events (opened, synchronize, ready_for_review, reopened). Runs anthropics/claude-code-action with the code-review plugin to provide AI-powered PR reviews. Uses GITHUB_TOKEN for PR write access.

nodes.name: Claude Code
nodes.summary: On-demand Claude Code Action pipeline triggered by issue comments, PR review comments, issue creation, or PR reviews containing '@claude' mention. Runs anthropics/claude-code-action to respond interactively to natural language requests. Has read-only permissions plus actions:read for CI context.

properties.summary: Master handoff document providing full context for LLM agents continuing work on the ccbot_ju1ian fork. Covers project goal (Telegram bot for Claude Code session monitoring via tmux), completed REF-001..009 refactoring, current state, remaining work, key architectural decisions (topic-only, window @id routing, auth invariants), artifacts listing, and agent playbook.
properties.purpose: Agent-to-agent handoff context for ccbot refactoring pipeline continuity
decisions: Window names only for display / re-resolve
Draft PR only; never push main from agent by default
Path allow/deny in config for blast radius control
invariants: No message truncation at parse layer
RetryAfter always re-raised from send paths

properties.summary: Specialized handoff for REF-006 task converting callback data from string parsing to typed frozen dataclasses. Documents branch agentic/REF-006-data-to-dataclasses, completed changes (frozen dataclasses, validation, round-trip serialization, 64-byte limit checks), and next steps after merge.
properties.purpose: Task-specific handoff for REF-006 callback data refactoring
sections: Artifacts and files modified by REF-006

properties.summary: Comprehensive implementation plan for the ccbot agentic pipeline. Covers end-to-end automation from knowledge graph analysis to validated draft PRs. Includes task structuring format, Grok (xAI) integration points, GitHub Actions workflow matrix, sub-agent roles, MCP server design, phased implementation roadmap, risk mitigations, and definitions of done.
properties.purpose: Reference architecture and execution plan for ccbot agentic pipeline
sections: Automation cycle (A-G stages with human gates)

properties.summary: Concise README for the ccbot agentic pipeline. Documents stages (ANALYZE → PLAN → IMPLEMENT → VALIDATE → draft PR), local CLI commands, directory layout, MCP server registration for Grok, secrets/permissions reference, and safety model (draft PR only, path allow/deny, quality gates).
properties.purpose: Quick-start documentation for the agentic pipeline

properties.summary: Main configuration for the agentic pipeline. Defines knowledge graph paths, backlog path, output directory structure, prioritization weights (critical:100, high:70, medium:40, low:15), implementation settings (branch prefix: agentic/, draft PR: true, labels), guardrails (allowed/ignored/blocked path globs, blocked commands, max LOC delta: 2000), and trigger schedules.
properties.purpose: Central configuration for pipeline behavior, weights, guardrails, and outputs

properties.summary: Hard guardrail rules for agentic implementers (Claude Code Action / local agents). Defines 8 must-rules (respect AGENTS.md, stable public behavior, run quality gates, add tests, small diffs, module docstrings, preserve patterns, no secrets) and 8 must-not rules (no non-topic routing, no window name routing, no early truncation, no auth weakening, no force-push, no CI gate modification, no arbitrary scripts, no safe token handling changes). Includes scope rules (single task, one PR per task) and review checklist.
properties.purpose: Hard constraints and review checklist for agentic code modification

properties.summary: Prompt for the ANALYZE stage: read-only analysis of the ccbot codebase using knowledge graph and source tree, producing a structured analysis report. Cross-checks backlog tasks against current code reality and identifies hotspots (complexity, fan-in/fan-out, untested files).
properties.rules: Read-only for src/ and tests/; prefer evidence from graph + file sizes; flag partially-done backlog tasks.

properties.summary: Prompt for the PLAN stage: turns analysis + backlog into an executable plan. Ranks ready tasks by priority, dependencies, and graph hotspots; selects one next task; expands acceptance criteria into a concrete implementation checklist.
selectionRules: Never pick a task whose depends_on are incomplete
Prefer high priority + high graph signal
Skip tasks covered by open agentic PRs
Default: one task per implementation run

properties.summary: Prompt for the IMPLEMENT stage: implements exactly one refactoring task using explore-design-implement-test-commit flow. Includes quality gates (ruff, pyright, pytest) and hard constraints against scope creep, force-pushing, or weakening auth/topic architecture.
hardConstraints: Do not implement multiple backlog tasks in one PR
Do not force-push or alter main directly
Do not weaken auth, topic-only architecture, or CI gates

properties.summary: GitHub PR body template for agentic refactoring PRs. Includes task metadata table, problem/solution sections, acceptance criteria checklist, validation gates, risk notes, and agent notes. Uses mustache-style {{PLACEHOLDER}} variables for dynamic content.
validationGates: Manual reasoning: topic-only + auth invariants preserved

properties.summary: Prompt for the REVIEW stage: reviews agentic PRs as a senior Python engineer. Focuses on correctness vs task/acceptance criteria, architecture constraints, concurrency/error-handling regressions, test adequacy, and scope creep. Outputs structured review with verdict.
focusAreas: Correctness vs task id and acceptance criteria
antiPatterns: Rewriting the PR in review-only mode
Approving if quality gates fail or auth/topic constraints violated

properties.summary: High-level system architecture covering the Telegram bot module layout, handler modules, state files, and key design decisions including topic-centric routing, window ID-centric state, hook-based session tracking, tool_use↔tool_result pairing, MarkdownV2 with fallback, and startup re-resolution.

Per-user message queue architecture with FIFO ordering, content/status merging, rate limiting via AIORateLimiter, mtime cache for poll efficiency, byte-offset incremental reads, and no-truncation policy at parse layer.

Exclusive Telegram Forum (topics) mode architecture defining 1 Topic = 1 Window = 1 Session mapping, thread_bindings (Topic→WindowID), session_map.json (WindowID→SessionID), message flows for outbound/inbound/new/resume sessions, topic lifecycle, and session lifecycle cleanup.
Telegram Bot main module: topic-based routing, /history, /screenshot, /esc, text forwarding, interactive UI handling, per-user message queue, MarkdownV2 output
Markdown → MarkdownV2 conversion with expandable quotes
Message sending: split_message (4096 char limit), rate_limit_send, safe_reply/safe_edit/safe_send with fallback
Terminal output parser: detect interactive UIs (AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission), parse status line (spinner + working text)
SessionMonitor: polls JSONL every 2s, detects mtime changes, parses new lines, tracks pending tools across poll cycles
TmuxManager: list/find/create/kill windows, send_keys to pane, capture_pane for screenshot
TranscriptParser: parse JSONL entries, pair tool_use↔tool_result, format expandable quotes, extract history
SessionManager: window↔session resolution, thread bindings (topic→window), message history retrieval, startup re-resolution
MonitorState: track byte offset per JSONL, prevent duplicate processing after restart
Hook: receive hook stdin from Claude Code SessionStart, write session_map.json
Terminal text→PNG rendering with ANSI color support and font fallback
Voice-to-text transcription via OpenAI API (gpt-4o-transcribe)
CLI entry point
Shared utilities: ccbot_dir, atomic_write_json
safe_reply/safe_edit/safe_send helpers with rate_limit_send
Per-user queue + worker with message merging and status dedup
Background status line polling at 1-second interval
Response pagination and formatting
Interactive UI: AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, Permission handling
Directory selection + session picker UI for new topic creation
Topic state cleanup on close/delete
Callback data constants
Persistent state: thread_bindings, window_states, display_names, read_offsets
Hook-generated window_id→session_id mapping (format: tmux_session:window_id)
Monitor poll progress: byte offset per JSONL file
Core mapping: each Telegram topic binds to one tmux window which hosts one Claude Code session
user_id → {thread_id → window_id} mapping stored in memory + state.json for routing user messages to tmux windows
tool_use_id tracked across poll cycles; tool_result edits the original tool_use Telegram message in-place
FIFO queue per user with worker that merges consecutive content messages, breaks on tool_use/tool_result, and handles status dedup
AIORateLimiter(max_retries=5) on Application (30/s global), 429 handling with retry_after, pre-filled bucket on restart
resolve_stale_ids() matches persisted display names against live windows to re-map window IDs after tmux server restart

nodes.description: Intermediate metadata file for understand-anything pipeline batch 5. Catalogs 8 root-level project files: .env.example, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, README_CN.md, README_RU.md, pyproject.toml, test.txt. These are top-level documentation and configuration files.

nodes.label: changed-files.txt
nodes.description: Flat space-separated list of changed/modified file paths across the entire project. Contains over 200 paths covering .agentic/, .github/workflows/, .grok/, .understand-anything/, src/, tests/, and root-level files. Used by the understand-anything pipeline to track which files have been modified and need re-analysis.
nodes.name: .understand-anything/tmp/changed-files.txt

nodes.label: import-map-input.json
nodes.description: Complete project-wide import map used by the understand-anything pipeline. Lists all 67 project files with their fileCategory (code, config, docs) and language (python, markdown, json, yaml, toml, txt, otf). Includes all source files under src/ccbot/ (core bot, handlers, utilities), tests/, config files, and documentation. Serves as the master file registry for the analysis pipeline.
nodes.name: .understand-anything/tmp/import-map-input.json

edges.label: parent handoff for REF-006 task detail
references implementation plan for task order
references config for weights and guardrails
references handoff document as parent context
references config for path and settings
sibling config files defining pipeline behavior
plan references REF-006 as example task
README summarizes the full implementation plan

properties.summary: ANALYZE stage produces analysis-report.json which PLAN stage consumes to rank and select tasks.
PLAN stage produces selected-task.json which IMPLEMENT stage consumes as the exact task to execute.
IMPLEMENT stage creates a GitHub PR, which REVIEW stage evaluates against acceptance criteria and quality gates.
IMPLEMENT stage references the PR body template (pr-body.md) to format the pull request description.
REVIEW stage evaluates the PR body (produced from pr-body.md template) against acceptance criteria.
Architecture overview references message queue and handling patterns
Architecture overview references topic-only design
Message handling doc references bot architecture for context
Topic architecture doc references overall architecture
Architecture doc describes bot.py as the main Telegram Bot module with topic routing, commands, and message forwarding
Architecture doc describes markdown_v2.py for MD→MarkdownV2 conversion with expandable quotes
Architecture doc describes telegram_sender.py for split_message and sending
Architecture doc describes terminal_parser.py for UI detection and status line parsing
Architecture doc describes SessionMonitor: polls JSONL every 2s, detects mtime changes, tracks pending tools
Architecture doc describes TmuxManager for window management and pane capture
Architecture doc describes TranscriptParser for JSONL parsing and tool_use↔tool_result pairing
Architecture doc describes SessionManager for window↔session resolution, thread bindings, and message history
Architecture doc describes MonitorState for byte offset tracking and duplicate prevention
Architecture doc describes Hook for receiving SessionStart hook and writing session_map.json
Architecture doc lists screenshot.py for terminal→PNG rendering
Architecture doc lists transcribe.py for voice-to-text via OpenAI
Architecture doc lists main.py as CLI entry point
Architecture doc lists utils.py for shared utilities (ccbot_dir, atomic_write_json)
Architecture doc lists message_sender.py handler for safe_reply/safe_edit/safe_send
Architecture doc lists message_queue.py handler for per-user queue and worker
Architecture doc lists status_polling.py handler for background status line polling
Architecture doc lists response_builder.py handler for response pagination and formatting
Architecture doc lists interactive_ui.py handler for AskUserQuestion/ExitPlanMode/Permission UI
Architecture doc lists directory_browser.py handler for directory selection and session picker
Architecture doc lists cleanup.py handler for topic state cleanup
Architecture doc lists callback_data.py handler for callback data constants
Architecture doc describes state.json for thread bindings, window states, display names, read offsets
Architecture doc describes session_map.json for hook-generated window_id→session mapping
Architecture doc describes monitor_state.json for poll progress tracking
Architecture doc establishes 1 topic = 1 window = 1 session as core design principle
Architecture doc defines tool_use_id tracking across poll cycles for in-place editing
Architecture doc defines resolve_stale_ids() for window ID remapping after tmux restart
Topic architecture doc formalizes the 1 Topic = 1 Window = 1 Session mapping with two mapping layers
Topic architecture doc defines thread_bindings as user_id→{thread_id→window_id} mapping stored in state.json
Topic architecture doc references SessionManager which manages thread_bindings
Topic architecture doc references session_map.json as key mapping from window_id to session_id
Topic architecture doc references SessionMonitor which uses session_map.json to decide which sessions to watch
Topic architecture doc references directory browser for new topic creation flow
Topic architecture doc references cleanup for topic lifecycle management
Message handling doc defines per-user FIFO queue with content merging, tool_use/tool_result breaks
Message handling doc defines AIORateLimiter configuration and 429 handling
Message handling doc describes the per-user queue implementation in message_queue.py
Message handling doc references status_polling.py for background terminal status polling
Message handling doc references safe_reply/safe_edit/safe_send for no-truncation send-layer split
Message handling doc references split_message for 4096-char splitting at send layer
Topic→Window binding is persisted in state.json via thread_bindings
Window→Session mapping is persisted in session_map.json via hook
thread_bindings are stored in state.json and loaded into memory on startup
Per-user queue + worker with merge logic is implemented in message_queue.py

edges.label: project config feeds source code
edges.description: batch-1 references pyproject.toml (project configuration) which defines dependencies and project metadata used by the Python source code files in batch-3

tour.title: Project Overview вЂ” Telegram Bot for Claude Code via tmux
tour.description: ccbot bridges Telegram forum topics to Claude Code sessions running inside tmux windows. Each topic maps 1-to-1 to a session. The README and architecture doc provide the big picture.

tour.title: Entry Point & Configuration
tour.description: The CLI entry point in main.py parses args and wires everything together. pyproject.toml defines dependencies (python-telegram-bot, libtmux, Pillow, httpx). Config loads .env for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and ALLOWED_USERS.

tour.title: Bot Application Core
tour.description: create_bot() in bot.py sets up the Application, registers handlers, and manages lifecycle (post_init / post_shutdown). Auth helpers gate access. Rate limiting (AIORateLimiter) protects against Telegram 429s. Startup re-resolution remaps stale window IDs after tmux restart.

tour.title: Session Management вЂ” Bindings & State
tour.description: SessionManager resolves topics to windows and windows to sessions. thread_bindings (topicв†’window_id) live in memory and persist in state.json. session_map.json (written by the hook) maps window_idв†’session_id. Dual-state design enables clean startup recovery.

tour.title: Session Monitoring вЂ” Polling & Offset Tracking
tour.description: SessionMonitor polls JSONL transcripts every 2s, detects mtime changes, reads new lines incrementally. MonitorState tracks byte offsets per file to avoid re-processing. tool_useв†”tool_result pairing tracks pending tool calls across poll cycles for in-place message edits.

tour.title: Tmux Integration вЂ” Windows, Hooks & Parsing
tour.description: TmuxManager wraps libtmux to list/find/create/kill windows, send keystrokes, and capture pane output. The hook script receives Claude Code SessionStart events via stdin and writes session_map.json. TerminalParser detects interactive UI prompts (AskUserQuestion, Permission, ExitPlanMode). TranscriptParser parses JSONL entries and formats expandable quotes.

tour.title: Message Pipeline вЂ” Sending, Formatting & Queuing
tour.description: telegram_sender.py handles split_message (4096-char limit) and rate-limited safe_send/safe_edit/safe_reply with MarkdownV2 fallback. markdown_v2.py converts Markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2 with expandable quotes. A per-user message queue (message_queue.py) merges consecutive content, breaks on tool boundaries, and deduplicates status updates.

tour.title: Handlers вЂ” Interactive UI, Browsing & Routing
tour.description: Inline keyboard handlers manage directory browsing (directory_browser.py), session picker, interactive UI prompts (interactive_ui.py), history pagination (response_builder.py), status polling (status_polling.py), and topic cleanup (cleanup.py). The callback_router dispatches all CB_* prefixed queries to the correct handler.

tour.title: Testing & CI Quality Gates
tour.description: The check.yml pipeline enforces ruff lint/format, pyright type checking, and pytest across Python 3.12/3.13. Unit tests cover config, session, monitor_state, transcript_parser, terminal_parser, telegram_sender, markdown_v2, hook, transcribe, and all handlers. Integration tests validate config loading and monitor state persistence.

tour.title: Beyond вЂ” Agentic Pipeline & Extensions
tour.description: The .agentic/ system provides an automated refactoring pipeline (analyzeв†’planв†’implementв†’validateв†’draft PR) driven by the knowledge graph. The CLI (scripts/agentic/cli.py) and MCP server expose pipeline tools to Grok. Screenshot rendering (screenshot.py) captures terminal output as PNG. Voice transcription (transcribe.py) supports gpt-4o-transcribe for voice messages.
